sections.paragraph:
συνελαύνω 1 aor. συνήλασα. Pass.: aor. ptc. gen. pl. συνελασθέντων 2 Macc 5:5; plpf. 3 sg. συνήλαστο 2 Macc 4:26 (Hom. et al.; pap; 2 Macc; Jos., Bell. 2, 526; 4, 567, Ant. 2, 249; 5, 162 [all four times w. εἰς and local acc.]) to cause movement through constraint, drive, force, bring εἴς τι to someth., in imagery (Aelian, VH 4, 15 εἰς τὸν τῆς σοφίας ἔρωτα; Sb 5357, 13 ς. τινὰ πρὸς εὐγνωμοσύνην=make someone reasonable) Ac 7:26 v.l.; GJs 18:3 v.l. (for ἀπελ.).—DELG s.v. ἐλαύνω.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. pl. = plural

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

someth. someth. = something

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 968
